name: va_1g_valid_resolves_to_spec_va_2m_valid
reason: `va_1g_valid` has `when_used_as_spec(spec_va_2m_valid)` instead of `spec_va_1g_valid` — a copy-paste bug that makes 1GB validation silently use the 2MB check in spec context

name: va_1g_accepts_2m_aligned_not_1g
code:
proof fn phi_2_va_1g_accepts_2m_aligned_not_1g(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_2m_valid(va),
        !spec_va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        va_1g_valid(va),
{
}
reason: Due to the wrong `when_used_as_spec`, a 2MB-aligned but non-1GB-aligned address passes `va_1g_valid`, allowing misaligned addresses into 1GB page table operations

name: v2l4index_precond_weakened_by_1g_bug
code:
proof fn phi_3_v2l4index_precond_weakened_by_1g_bug(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_2m_valid(va),
        !spec_va_1g_valid(va),
        !spec_va_4k_valid(va),
    ensures
        va_4k_valid(va) || va_2m_valid(va) || va_1g_valid(va),
{
}
reason: `v2l4index`'s precondition is weaker than intended — any 2MB-valid address satisfies the disjunction via the broken `va_1g_valid` path even when it's not truly 1GB-aligned

name: v2l4index_lower_bound_is_one
code:
proof fn phi_4_v2l4index_lower_bound_is_one(va: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va) || va_2m_valid(va) || va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l4index(va) >= 1,
{
}
reason: `KERNEL_MEM_END_L4INDEX = 1` means L4 index 0 is excluded — the entire first 512GB of virtual address space is blocked, but index 1 (starting at 512GB) may still be within user-space range on some OS configurations

name: spec_va_1g_valid_implies_2m_valid
reason: 1GB alignment implies 2MB alignment since the 1GB mask is stricter — this means the `va_1g_valid` copy-paste bug makes the 1GB and 2MB spec paths fully equivalent, completely hiding the missing 1GB check
